value: Headline: Crude sold off hard into Monday's close — WTI settling $74.14, down $1.71 — even as last week's WPSR confirmed a Cushing hub in genuine distress, with the delivery point sitting at 20.0 mb, already below the ~25 mb operational floor. The push-pull is stark: a screaming 8.3 mb crude draw against a market that has unwound its post-Iran fear premium. Tight physical fundamentals are colliding with a macro narrative of 'crude calm,' and for now the calm is winning the flat price. Crude & products: The latest weekly EIA report (week ending June 12, 2026) showed a massive 8.3 mb crude draw to 418.2 mb, with refiners running flat-out — utilization up 1.4 points to 96.7% and crude inputs at 17.19 mb/d. Cushing fell another 1.6 mb to 20.0 mb, now down 5.8 mb over five weeks from 25.82 mb on May 15 — operating well below tank-bottom minimums, with deliverability stress only deepening. The draw was front-loaded in PADD II (-4.5 mb) and PADD III (-4.8 mb) as Gulf Coast and Midwest plants ran near peak. On products, gasoline drew 0.9 mb to 214.2 mb and jet drew 1.1 mb to 45.0 mb, while distillate built 1.0 mb to 103.1 mb — total stocks fell 9.4 mb to 780.5 mb. Refining margins: Margins remain robust even as flat price slides — the 3:2:1 crack sits at $49.83, with the distillate crack at $54.82 leading the gasoline crack at $47.34 and jet at $42.32. Those margins explain the 96.7% utilization print: refiners are incentivized to chase product cracks, which is precisely what gutted crude stocks last week. RBOB settled $2.8924/gal (-1c) and ULSD $3.0705/gal (-1c), both easing in sympathy with crude rather than on any product-specific weakness. Curve structure: WTI holds a backwardated M1-M12 spread of $5.26 with a front M1-M2 of $0.82 — a curve still pricing prompt tightness consistent with sub-floor Cushing. Brent backwardation is shallower at $4.99 M1-M12 and just $0.39 M1-M2. The narrow Brent-WTI spread of $4.00 is the structural worry: with WTI only $4 under Brent, the US export arb is weak, barrels stay home, and that should pressure domestic builds — yet Cushing-specific deliverability is keeping the WTI prompt bid. To restore export competitiveness, WTI needs to cheapen relative to Brent and re-widen the spread. News & geopolitics: The macro story is the unwind of the post-Iran conflict risk premium — multiple desks now frame the tape as 'crude calm,' with majors like Chevron and ConocoPhillips drawing analyst attention as flat price slips and Hormuz fears fade. That fits The New York Times read that US oil is skipping its chance to grab Gulf market share — the narrow arb means American barrels aren't displacing OPEC exports, even as Saudi April crude exports fell to a record low per JODI data, which should be supportive but isn't translating to WTI strength. The UAE's reported OPEC exit chatter adds another layer of supply-side uncertainty to a cartel already managing fragile cohesion. On the demand and supply-build side, Sky Quarry firing up Nevada's only refinery adds marginal West Coast capacity into a region structurally short, while pump prices are rolling over broadly — Erie dropping below $4/gal and nationwide diesel falling. The takeaway for today: physical tightness (sub-floor Cushing, 8.3 mb draw, 96.7% runs) is real, but until the risk-premium bleed stops, flat price stays heavy and the cracks — not crude — remain where the money is.
